prompt: Play this short three-step World 7 depth synthesis chain.

steps.prompt: Flop: Medium depth keeps future-street pressure alive. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This medium-depth flop step prefers the controlled line.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. With medium depth still leaving room to pressure later streets, this flop should stay on the controlled continue instead of jumping to aggression.
steps.why_v1: When later streets still matter but the edge is not yet strong enough to press, controlled continuation keeps the line cleaner.

steps.prompt: Turn: Deep depth and position now support more pressure. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This deeper turn step supports a raise.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Once deeper stacks and position both improve the pressure edge, this turn should lean into the stronger raise.
steps.why_v1: When depth and position both improve the pressure edge, the learner should stop defaulting to passive continuation.

steps.prompt: River: Shallow closure and a thin edge should end the pressure line. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This shallow closure step should release.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. When the stack is shallow enough that the pressure edge is gone, this river should release rather than force one more continue.
steps.why_v1: When remaining depth is no longer giving useful leverage and the edge is thin, disciplined release is cleaner than forced continuation.
